description: Create the complete Electron HDR Video Analyzer application with all source files. This is a single comprehensive feature that creates the entire app structure including: Electron main/renderer processes, the HDR analysis engine (ported from Python), chart generation using node-canvas/chartjs-node-canvas, and the UI for file selection and image display/save.
steps: Create electron-app/ directory with package.json containing all dependencies: electron, electron-builder, ffmpeg-static, fluent-ffmpeg, chartjs-node-canvas (for server-side chart rendering), chart.js, and chartjs-plugin-annotation
Create electron-app/main.js - Electron main process: creates BrowserWindow, handles IPC for file dialog (open video), save dialog (save image), and triggers analysis in a worker/child process
Create electron-app/preload.js - Exposes safe IPC bridge to renderer using contextBridge: selectVideo(), saveImage(buffer), onAnalysisProgress(callback), onAnalysisComplete(callback)
Create electron-app/index.html - Simple UI with: a 'Select Video' button, a progress indicator, an image display area (canvas or img tag), and a 'Save As' button. Clean modern styling with CSS.
Create electron-app/renderer.js - Frontend logic: handles button clicks, calls preload APIs, displays progress, shows resulting chart image, enables save button when done

Create electron-app/analysis/hdr-analyzer.js - Core analysis engine ported from Python: PQ EOTF conversion, Rec.2020 luminance calculation, gamut classification (is_in_gamut triangle test), ffprobe duration detection, ffmpeg rawvideo pipe reading (gbrp10le 10-bit 3840x2160), frame-by-frame processing with progress reporting. Uses child_process.spawn for ffmpeg, reads stdout pipe as raw Buffer, processes uint16 values.

Create electron-app/analysis/chart-generator.js - Chart generation ported from Python's matplotlib logic: 3-panel vertical chart (brightness over time with PQ axis, gamut ratio stacked area, APL histogram). Uses node-canvas directly to draw the charts (matplotlib-style custom rendering with Canvas 2D API). Includes PQ inverse function for Y-axis tick placement, stat text boxes (MaxCLL, AveCLL, MaxFALL, AveFALL, Average/Median APL).
Create electron-app/build-config.js or update package.json 'build' section with electron-builder config that bundles ffmpeg-static binary with the app using extraResources
Create a README.md inside electron-app/ with setup instructions: npm install, npm start, npm run build (for packaging)
acceptance_criteria: electron-app/package.json is valid JSON with all required dependencies listed
All JS files pass node --check syntax validation
electron-app/main.js properly creates a BrowserWindow and sets up IPC handlers for selectVideo, saveImage, and startAnalysis
electron-app/analysis/hdr-analyzer.js contains correct PQ EOTF implementation matching Python constants (m1=2610/16384, m2=2523/4096*128, c1=3424/4096, c2=2413/4096*32, c3=2392/4096*32)
electron-app/analysis/hdr-analyzer.js contains correct Rec.2020 luminance coefficients [0.262700, 0.677998, 0.059302]
electron-app/analysis/hdr-analyzer.js contains correct gamut vertices for Rec.709 and DCI-P3 matching the Python source
electron-app/analysis/chart-generator.js generates a 3-panel chart image (brightness, gamut ratio, APL histogram)
The UI has a file selection button, progress display, image display area, and save-as functionality
electron-app/README.md exists with clear setup and usage instructions
verification: Run 'node --check electron-app/renderer.js' to verify syntax (may need adjustment for browser context)
Verify all files referenced in package.json main field exist
findings: All 8 files created and syntax-validated with node --check. NODE_OPTIONS must be cleared to avoid sandbox preload module interference. Package.json validated as correct JSON with main field pointing to existing main.js. The sandbox environment uses INTEGRATIONS_ONLY so npm install cannot be run, but all source code is structurally correct.
